name: Get promotion details (GET 200)
description: Documented Promotion output-representation response. The Promotion schema (doc p.604) wraps promotionDetails (Unified Promotions output, JSON example doc p.620), rules (Template Configuration[]), and promotionConfigurationWarnings (String[]). The promotionDetails object below is copied verbatim from the documented Unified Promotions JSON example.
notes: SOURCE: Loyalty Mgmt Developer Guide v67.0 (Summer 26), /tmp/loyalty_api.txt. Resource definition "Promotion Details (GET, PUT)" at lines 24183-24199: Resource=/global-promotions-management/promotions/promotionId, Available version=60.0, HTTP methods="GET, PUT". DISCREPANCY: the task target labeled this "Get / Update / Delete" but the authoritative doc lists ONLY GET and PUT for this resource — NO DELETE method is documented anywhere for /promotions/{promotionId}. I set method=GET per the expected method and added the PUT fixture; do not implement DELETE without a doc source. The doc does NOT print a dedicated JSON response example inside the GET/PUT resource block; it names the response schema as "Promotion" (the doc's adjacent configuration resource shows "Response body for GET -> Promotion" at line 24144). The "Promotion" Response Body schema (line 32491-32504) has exactly three properties: promotionConfigurationWarnings (String[]), promotionDetails (Unified Promotions Output[]), rules (Template Configuration[]). I populated promotionDetails verbatim from the documented "Unified Promotions" JSON example (lines 33409-33497). NOTE: in that example promotionDetails is a single object, but the Promotion schema types it as an array (Unified Promotions Output[]) — I wrapped it in an array to match the schema typing; an implementer may prefer a bare object to match the raw example. The PUT request uses root XML tag unifiedPromotionsInputRepresentation; request property table at lines 24335-24425 (name=Required, startDateTime=Required, others Optional; loyaltyProgram cannot be changed on an existing promotion). The Unified Promotions output table types loyaltyProgram as String, but the JSON example shows it as an object {id,name} — I kept the JSON-example form. No documented error/empty response example exists for this resource, so no error fixture was created. {promotionId} is a path placeholder (e.g. 0c8SB0000000rCPYAY style 15/18-char SF id).
